atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports experiencing bilateral knee pain

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports bilateral knee pain for two weeks following a skiing injury

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports pain is worse with prolonged walking

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports pain occasionally reaches a severity of eleven on a pain scale

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports a pop sensation in his left knee at the time of injury

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient denies any other hospitalizations or surgeries in the past

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports a previous reconstruction of his right knee at age fourteen due to a gymnastics injury

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient describes a history of being a 'tough' athlete with a high pain tolerance

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports consuming THC gummies from his grandmother

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Physical exam reveals mild swelling and bruising in both knees

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Gait reveals a slight limp

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Range of motion is generally intact, although the patient reports some stiffness in the morning

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Dorsiflexion causes pain, while plantar flexion does not

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient has a posterior cruciate ligament strain in the right knee

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports the right knee pain is exacerbated by previous injury

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports a limp

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The left knee appears unstable

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The left knee may be related to a prior reconstruction

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician prescribes meloxicam 15 mg daily for right knee pain

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician prescribes ibuprofen 800 mg twice daily for left knee pain

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician orders X-rays to evaluate for arthritis and rule out fracture

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician recommends continuing to brace the right knee to reduce stress

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician recommends physical therapy three times per week to improve strength and stability

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician recommends avoiding skiing and gymnastics activities for now

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician instructs monitoring the patient's symptoms and adjusting the plan as needed
